vuln_q_0003_0001.title: python.flask.security.audit.directly-returned-format-string.directly-returned-format-string
vuln_q_0003_0001.description: Detected Flask route directly returning a formatted string. This is subject to cross-site scripting if user input can reach the string. Consider using the template engine instead and rendering pages with 'render_template()'.

vuln_q_0003_0002.title: python.flask.security.audit.flask-url-for-external-true.flask-url-for-external-true
vuln_q_0003_0002.description: Function `flask.url_for` with `_external=True` argument will generate URLs using the `Host` header of the HTTP request, which may lead to security risks such as Host header injection

vuln_q_0003_0002.title: python.django.security.audit.unvalidated-password.unvalidated-password
vuln_q_0003_0002.description: The password on 'user' is being set without validating the password. Call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password() with validation functions before setting the password. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.0/topics/auth/passwords/ for more information.

vuln_q_0006_0000.title: python.lang.security.audit.insecure-file-permissions.insecure-file-permissions
vuln_q_0006_0000.description: These permissions `0o666` are widely permissive and grant access to more people than may be necessary. A good default is `0o644` which gives read and write access to yourself and read access to everyone else.

vuln_q_0006_0002.title: python.lang.security.audit.insecure-file-permissions.insecure-file-permissions
vuln_q_0006_0002.description: These permissions `0o666` are widely permissive and grant access to more people than may be necessary. A good default is `0o644` which gives read and write access to yourself and read access to everyone else.

vuln_q_0009_0000.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0009_0000.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0009_0000.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml-parse.use-defused-xml-parse
vuln_q_0009_0000.description: The native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks.  These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.

vuln_q_0009_0001.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0009_0001.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0009_0001.title: python.flask.security.audit.app-run-param-config.avoid_app_run_with_bad_host
vuln_q_0009_0001.description: Running flask app with host 0.0.0.0 could expose the server publicly.

vuln_q_0009_0003.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0009_0003.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0009_0004.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service. Do not use this library to parse untrusted input. Instead  the Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml`.

vuln_q_0012_0000.title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
vuln_q_0012_0000.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'run' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.

vuln_q_0012_0001.title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
vuln_q_0012_0001.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'run' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.

vuln_q_0012_0001.title: python.lang.security.audit.subprocess-shell-true.subprocess-shell-true
vuln_q_0012_0001.description: Found 'subprocess' function 'run' with 'shell=True'. This is dangerous because this call will spawn the command using a shell process. Doing so propagates current shell settings and variables, which makes it much easier for a malicious actor to execute commands. Use 'shell=False' instead.

vuln_q_0014_0004.title: python.flask.security.open-redirect.open-redirect
vuln_q_0014_0004.description: Data from request is passed to redirect(). This is an open redirect and could be exploited. Consider using 'url_for()' to generate links to known locations. If you must use a URL to unknown pages, consider using 'urlparse()' or similar and checking if the 'netloc' property is the same as your site's host name. See the references for more information.

vuln_q_0017_0000.title: python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string.render-template-string
vuln_q_0017_0000.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.

vuln_q_0020_0003.title: python.lang.security.audit.dynamic-urllib-use-detected.dynamic-urllib-use-detected
vuln_q_0020_0003.description: Detected a dynamic value being used with urllib. urllib supports 'file://' schemes, so a dynamic value controlled by a malicious actor may allow them to read arbitrary files. Audit uses of urllib calls to ensure user data cannot control the URLs, or consider using the 'requests' library instead.

vuln_q_0025_0001.title: python.lang.security.audit.httpsconnection-detected.httpsconnection-detected
vuln_q_0025_0001.description: The HTTPSConnection API has changed frequently with minor releases of Python. Ensure you are using the API for your version of Python securely. For example, Python 3 versions prior to 3.4.3 will not verify SSL certificates by default. See https://docs.python.org/3/library/http.client.html#http.client.HTTPSConnection for more information.

vuln_q_0026_0001.title: python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string.render-template-string

vuln_q_0026_0001.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.

vuln_q_0029_0001.title: python.flask.security.injection.subprocess-injection.subprocess-injection
vuln_q_0029_0001.description: Detected user input entering a `subprocess` call unsafely. This could result in a command injection vulnerability. An attacker could use this vulnerability to execute arbitrary commands on the host, which allows them to download malware, scan sensitive data, or run any command they wish on the server. Do not let users choose the command to run. In general, prefer to use Python API versions of system commands. If you must use subprocess, use a dictionary to allowlist a set of commands.

vuln_q_0029_0001.title: python.lang.security.dangerous-subprocess-use.dangerous-subprocess-use
vuln_q_0029_0001.description: Detected subprocess function 'run' with user controlled data. A malicious actor could leverage this to perform command injection. You may consider using 'shlex.escape()'.

vuln_q_0029_0001.title: python.flask.security.audit.render-template-string.render-template-string
vuln_q_0029_0001.description: Found a template created with string formatting. This is susceptible to server-side template injection and cross-site scripting attacks.

vuln_q_0031_0001.description: The password on 'new_user' is being set without validating the password. Call django.contrib.auth.password_validation.validate_password() with validation functions before setting the password. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.0/topics/auth/passwords/ for more information.

vuln_q_0031_0002.title: python.flask.security.audit.debug-enabled.debug-enabled
vuln_q_0031_0002.description: Detected Flask app with debug=True. Do not deploy to production with this flag enabled as it will leak sensitive information. Instead, consider using Flask configuration variables or setting 'debug' using system environment variables.

vuln_q_0046_0004.title: python.cryptography.security.mode-without-authentication.crypto-mode-without-authentication
vuln_q_0046_0004.description: An encryption mode of operation is being used without proper message authentication. This can potentially result in the encrypted content to be decrypted by an attacker. Consider instead use an AEAD mode of operation like GCM.

vuln_q_0046_0004.title: python.cryptography.security.mode-without-authentication.crypto-mode-without-authentication
vuln_q_0046_0004.description: An encryption mode of operation is being used without proper message authentication. This can potentially result in the encrypted content to be decrypted by an attacker. Consider instead use an AEAD mode of operation like GCM.

vuln_q_0068_0001.title: python.lang.security.use-defused-xml.use-defused-xml
vuln_q_0068_0001.description: The Python documentation recommends using `defusedxml` instead of `xml` because the native Python `xml` library is vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) attacks. These attacks can leak confidential data and "XML bombs" can cause denial of service.

vuln_q_0068_0002.title: python.lang.security.audit.eval-detected.eval-detected
vuln_q_0068_0002.description: Detected the use of eval(). eval() can be dangerous if used to evaluate dynamic content. If this content can be input from outside the program, this may be a code injection vulnerability. Ensure evaluated content is not definable by external sources.

vuln_q_0069_0000.title: python.pycryptodome.security.insecure-cipher-algorithm-des.insecure-cipher-algorithm-des
vuln_q_0069_0000.description: Detected DES cipher or Triple DES algorithm which is considered insecure. This algorithm is not cryptographically secure and can be reversed easily. Use a secure symmetric cipher from the cryptodome package instead. Use secure stream ciphers such as ChaCha20, XChaCha20 and Salsa20, or a block cipher such as AES with a block size of 128 bits. When using a block cipher, use a modern mode of operation that also provides authentication, such as GCM.

vuln_q_0069_0004.title: python.lang.security.insecure-hash-algorithms-md5.insecure-hash-algorithm-md5
vuln_q_0069_0004.description: Detected MD5 hash algorithm which is considered insecure. MD5 is not collision resistant and is therefore not suitable as a cryptographic signature. Use SHA256 or SHA3 instead.

vuln_q_0077_0000.title: python.lang.security.deserialization.pickle.avoid-pickle
vuln_q_0077_0000.description: Avoid using `pickle`, which is known to lead to code execution vulnerabilities. When unpickling, the serialized data could be manipulated to run arbitrary code. Instead, consider serializing the relevant data as JSON or a similar text-based serialization format.
